references.title: Predicting Student Performance in Online Learning: A Multidimensional Time-Series Data Analysis Approach

extracted.Target: Distinction vs Pass vs Fail vs Withdraw
extracted.Courses: 4 courses in STEM and 3 couses in Social Sciences
extracted.Features: [Folder, Forumng, Ouollaborate, Oucontent, Ouwiki, Ouelluminate, Quiz, Questionnaires, Dataplus, Dualpane, Homepage, Htmlactivity, Page, Resource, RepeatActivity, Glossary, Url, Subpage, Shartedsubpage, Externalquiz, Code_module, Code_presentation, Gender, Region, Highest Education, Imd_band, Age_band, Num_of_prev_attempts, Studied_credits, Disability]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors “normalized” course duration as 270 days. 

The presented model receives a dataset containing multiple multi-variate time series (each timeseries consists of a student in a course module edition). This dataset is passed through 3-layer LSTM, whose output is passed to a Dropout layer (into an attention layer).

The embedding resulting from the Attention module is the input to the “final” MLP.
extracted.Models: RF, Naive Bayes, MLP, SEPN, MTAPSP
extracted.Comments:
The authors’ work is done in multiple stages: 

1. One experiment evaluating class specific metrics and no early prediction comparing multiple “candidate algorithms” - MTAPSP wings on all acounts.
extracted.Limitations:
- Reports mentioned in Abstract are misleading - they refer to Wi-FD on 100% course duration.

- Holdout method

- Only tested in OULAD

extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors “normalized” course duration as 270 days. 

The presented model receives a dataset containing multiple multi-variate time series (each timeseries consists of a student in a course module edition). This dataset is passed through 3-layer LSTM, whose output is passed to a Dropout layer (into an attention layer).

The embedding resulting from the Attention module is the input to the “final” MLP. v
extracted.Comments:
2. Authors do different forms of classification at different stages of course completion:

WF-PD: Pass-Fail
F-PD: Pass vs Fail (excluding dropouts)
W-PD: Dropout vs Pass

3. Ablation studies to see effects of the different layers in the overall predictive process.
 
2. forms of representation are considered: Daily and Weekly,
extracted.Limitations:
- Reports mentioned in Abstract are misleading - they refer to Wi-FD on 100% course duration.

- Holdout method

- Only tested in OULAD

annotations.text: Online learning has become increasingly popular even though it lacks formal communication channels for feedback. Authorsd propose a multi-dimensional time-series model that is able to consider multiple features of students: behavior, characteristics and even prior performance.

Results show that the model achieved close to 100% accuracy (?) on OULAD in the task of early detection of students at risk. Moreover, the model achieves 74% and 74% F1 score in the multiclass problem of OULAD. As usual in these papers, model beats all other models it is being benchmarked against.
